steps.title: Identify the passage's central claim about Hoocąk
steps.content: The passage defines reduplication (repeating a root word element to form a related word), illustrates it with zi ("yellow") and zizi ("yellow in spots"), notes that the full word is repeated in this case, and ends with: "There are many examples of this type of reduplication in Hoocąk." The question asks which answer matches what the text actually asserts about Hoocąk.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice against what the passage states
steps.content:
• Choice A — It is one of the only languages in the world that makes use of reduplication.: The passage never claims Hoocąk is unique among world languages in using reduplication — it only discusses Hoocąk without comparing it to other languages. This claim goes beyond the text. ✗
  • Choice B — It is closely related to another language from the Midwest region of what is now the United States that also makes use of reduplication.: The passage mentions no other language at all, let alone a related one. This is entirely absent from the text. ✗
  • Choice C — It contains many instances of the type of reduplication seen in the words zi and zizi.: This paraphrases the passage's final sentence directly: "There are many examples of this type of reduplication in Hoocąk." The type referenced is full-word reduplication, illustrated by zi/zizi. ✓
  • Choice D — It has a wide variety of reduplicated words and phrases that mean "yellow.": The passage uses zi and zizi only as examples of reduplication, not to suggest Hoocąk has many yellow-related reduplications. The text never makes this claim. ✗
That is the correct answer.
